ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a dedicated term for skinless roasted whole turkey; closest defensible match is roasted turkey meat. URI used is an approximation; verified FoodOn term for cooked turkey meat used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LX covers cooked turkey meat broadly; no specific skinless-roasted sub-code exists. This is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for skinless roasted turkey as a food item; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of roasted turkey without skin is approximately 85 g (about 3 oz), equivalent to a palm-sized portion of sliced breast or thigh meat.

nutrient_source.title: Turkey, all classes, meat only, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 05178)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for roasted turkey, meat only (without skin), all classes. Values cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Food Composition Database entry for roasted turkey without skin (cr_id F11050007). Core macros and micronutrients are consistent between both sources; HPB values used as primary per source priority rules for Singapore records, with USDA used for extended micronutrient fields not reported by HPB.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: Roasted turkey without skin contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. The numeric value 0 is used here solely to satisfy the template requirement for a numeric field; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey that GI testing is not meaningful for this food. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (101 mg/100 g) with favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (239 mg K : 101 mg Na)
hypertension.guidance: Roasted turkey without skin is appropriate for hypertensive individuals; its sodium content of 101 mg/100 g is well within DASH dietary targets, and the potassium content of 239 mg/100 g supports vascular tone — avoid pre-basted or pre-seasoned commercial turkey products which may contain >400 mg sodium/100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high lean protein (29.1 g/100 g) supports glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0 g carbohydrate and 0 g sugar per 100 g, roasted turkey without skin exerts no glycaemic impact and is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the high protein content (29.1 g/100 g) promotes satiety and supports stable postprandial blood glucose — pair with low-GI vegetables and avoid starchy gravies or stuffing.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (3.84 g/100 g), low saturated fat (1.09 g/100 g), and zero trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Roasted turkey without skin is a lean protein source with low saturated fat (1.09 g/100 g) and zero trans fat, making it consistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns recommended by the American Heart Association; cholesterol content of 101 mg/100 g is moderate and acceptable within a balanced diet — avoid adding butter, cream-based sauces, or high-sodium gravies.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of positive anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, antioxidants, polyphenols); lean protein supports muscle maintenance
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Roasted turkey without skin is non-inflammatory due to very low saturated fat (~1.1 g/100 g) and zero trans fat, and its high protein (29.1 g/100 g) supports muscle maintenance important in RA management; however, it lacks positive anti-inflammatory factors such as omega-3 fatty acids, antioxidants, or polyphenols — pair with anti-inflammatory foods such as oily fish, turmeric, or cruciferous vegetables for a more RA-supportive meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted turkey without skin is a lean, high-protein cooked poultry product made from whole turkey that has been oven-roasted until fully cooked and then had its skin removed before consumption. The meat displays a light brown exterior with a tender, moist texture throughout, encompassing both white meat (breast) and dark meat (thigh, drumstick, wing) cuts. Per 100 g, it provides 159 kcal, 29.1 g protein, 3.84 g total fat (of which only 1.09 g is saturated), 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, 101 mg sodium, and 239 mg potassium. It is an excellent source of B vitamins — particularly niacin (9.93 mg), vitamin B6 (0.73 mg), and vitamin B12 (0.35 mcg) — as well as zinc (3.09 mg) and selenium (32 mcg). The absence of carbohydrate and sugar makes it suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. Its low sodium and favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio align with DASH dietary principles for hypertension management. The low saturated fat and zero trans fat profile make it appropriate for cardiovascular health. Turkey is widely consumed in Western and American culinary traditions, particularly associated with festive occasions such as Thanksgiving and Christmas, but is also eaten year-round as a lean protein staple. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free, and is halal when slaughtered according to Islamic dietary law. Clinically, it is a preferred lean protein for weight management, muscle preservation, and metabolic health.
common_user_queries: Is roasted turkey without skin healthy?
How many calories in roasted turkey without skin?
Is turkey good for high blood pressure?
Is skinless turkey low in fat?
What is the protein content of roasted turkey?
What vitamins are in turkey meat?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from Singapore HPB Food Composition Database (cr_id F11050007, confidence 0.95) with cross-validation against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for roasted turkey, meat only, all classes (NDB No. 05178). Both sources are approved primary sources. Values are consistent between sources. Extended micronutrient values sourced from USDA SR Legacy where HPB does not report them.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero: roasted turkey meat contains no carbohydrate by composition. Sugar_g is naturally zero: no sugars present in plain roasted turkey meat. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero: animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 05178). Vitamin_c_mg and vitamin_a_mcg_rae are 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy for cooked turkey meat without skin). Monounsaturated_fat_g (0.84 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.95 g), and all extended micronutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy where not reported by HPB. Halal status assumes slaughter according to Islamic dietary law; kosher status not confirmed. Glycemic index: this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g carbohydrate per 100 g); gi_category is set to not_applicable. The glycemic_index field is set to the numeric value 0 to satisfy template numeric-field requirements; this does not represent a measured GI value but rather indicates GI testing is not applicable for this negligible-carbohydrate food. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. No -1 sentinel values require documentation as all fields have been populated from approved sources.

All three mandatory health conditions present with specific clinical guidance. LLM description exceeds 200-character minimum. Alias consistency verified. No multilingual requirements apply as this is a Western/American food item.
